task_name: add Mistral Instruct v3 chat template

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's a summary of the complete plan:

---

## Plan: Add `mistral-instruct-v3` Chat Template Handler

**Single file modified**: `common/chat.cpp` only — no header or existing handler changes.

### 4 purely-additive changes:

**1. Template source constant** (after the existing `CHATML_TEMPLATE_SRC` macro, ~line 568)  
A `#define MISTRAL_INSTRUCT_V3_TEMPLATE_SRC` holding the canonical Mistral Instruct v0.3 Jinja template. It extracts a leading system message, prepends it only to the **first** user turn, wraps every user turn in `[INST] … [/INST]`, and appends `</s>` (via `eos_token`) after assistant turns.

**2. Named registration** (new `if` block before the existing `chatml` check in `common_chat_templates_init()`, ~line 613)  
```cpp
if (default_template_src == "mistral-instruct-v3") {
    default_template_src = MISTRAL_INSTRUCT_V3_TEMPLATE_SRC;
}
```  
Enables `--chat-template mistral-instruct-v3` to work without touching the enum or header.

**3. Handler function** (new static function before `common_chat_params_init_ministral_3()`, ~line 824)  
Renders the prompt via `common_chat_template_direct_apply(tmpl, inputs)` and sets `COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY` (no tool-call parsing for this simple format).

**4. Auto-detection** (new `if` block before `return std::nullopt;` in `common_chat_try_specialized_template()`, ~line 1689)  
```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos &&   // not Ministral 3
    src.find("[AVAILABLE_TOOLS]") == std::string::npos) { // not Mistral v3/v3-tekken
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: mistral-instruct-v3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```  
The double-absence check is exactly the described way to distinguish from the Ministral 3 handler (`[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` absent) while also excluding the tool-capable v3 templates.
